title: File Types Explained
description: Understand the most common file types used in the workplace so you always choose the right format for the task.
objectives: Identify common file types and their uses
Know when to use Word, PDF, Excel, or image formats
Understand file compatibility between different software
Recognize Publisher and Word Art formats
content:
Why File Types Matter

Not all files are the same. A file type determines what program opens it, how it looks, and whether others can edit it. In the workplace, using the wrong file type can cause confusion, formatting errors, or security risks.

Common Workplace File Types

File TypeBest ForNotes
.docxEditable documents, letters, reportsOpens in Word. Can be edited.
.pdfFinal documents, forms, invoicesLooks the same on every device. Harder to edit.
.xlsxBudgets, data, calculationsOpens in Excel. Supports formulas.
.jpg / .pngImages, logos, screenshotsPNG is better for text/screenshots.
.zipCompressing multiple filesUseful for sending large attachments.
.pubBrochures, flyers, newslettersOpens in Microsoft Publisher. For desktop publishing.

Workplace Scenario

You need to send a contract to a client. You wrote it in Word. Should you send the .docx file or a PDF? The correct answer is PDF. A PDF preserves your formatting exactly and prevents the client from accidentally changing the terms. If you need feedback on a draft, send the .docx. If it's final, send a PDF.

Word Art and Publisher Files

Microsoft Publisher (.pub) is used for creating brochures, newsletters, and business cards. Word Art is a feature inside Word and Publisher that turns text into decorative graphics. In the workplace, you might use Publisher to create a company newsletter or a flyer for an event. These files should usually be exported to PDF before sharing with people who do not have Publisher installed.

downloads.title: File Types Quick Reference
downloads.description: Printable chart of common file types

exercise: Create a new folder on your computer named File_Types_Practice. Inside it, create one example of each type: a Word document (.docx), a text file (.txt), and rename an image file to use .png extension. Write a sentence inside each explaining what it is best used for. Then create a simple table in Word listing 5 file types, their extensions, and when to use them.

quiz.question: You are sending a final invoice to a client. Which file type is best?
quiz.explanation: PDF preserves formatting and prevents accidental edits, making it ideal for final documents like invoices.

quiz.question: Which file type supports formulas and calculations?
quiz.explanation: Excel files (.xlsx) are designed for data, calculations, and formulas.

quiz.question: Why might you send a .docx instead of a PDF?
options: The recipient needs to edit the document
quiz.explanation: When collaboration and editing are needed, a Word document is the better choice.

quiz.question: Which software is best for creating a company newsletter or flyer?
quiz.explanation: Microsoft Publisher is designed for desktop publishing tasks like brochures, newsletters, and flyers.
